Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Got it lets tackle this step by step First I need to start by listing out the specific anomalies from the temporal profile first right Lets go through each of the flagged ones first make sure to explain whats off about each average and stdev

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First lets list the anomalies clearly 1 First anomaly is RP Average 25 hours 90000 seconds is 25 hours right 3600 25 90k yeah with extremely low STDEV of 1 hour

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Because R to P should go through A and E right The intended process is RAEP so that path has multiple steps yet the time from R to P is super consistent Thats weird almost like its a fixed deadline or automated without actual evaluation Also low stdev means almost all claims hit exactly that 25 hour mark which is odd for a process with multiple manual steps probably

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Second anomaly PN Average 7 days stdev 2 days Thats way too long for a notification step after approval right Notification is usually a quick automated step but here its a week on average with huge variance

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That suggests either the notification step is manually triggered and has backlogs or theres a system delay thats inconsistent or maybe approvals arent being communicated promptly because of resource issues

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Third AC average 2 hours stdev 1 hour Wait the intended process is AEPNC right So closing right after assignment skipping all the middle steps Thats a big red flag

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Because 2 hours isnt enough to do evaluation approval notification so these claims are being closed prematurely without proper processing The stdev is 1 hour so most of these are closed between 1 and 3 hours after assignment which is way too fast

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Fourth EN average 5 minutes stdev 1 minute Evaluate to notify is supposed to have approval in between right The process is EPN So 5 minutes is way too short to get approval

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: so that suggests that either the approval step is being skipped or its an automated approval that doesnt have actual review or the timestamps are wrong like evaluation and approval are merged or notify is triggered immediately without approval

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait also are there any other anomalies Lets check the others Like RE is 1 day stdev 8 hours thats high variance but maybe thats normal if some claims are simple some complex RA is 1 hour stdev 10 mins thats okay Wait no wait the example anomalies listed the four I mentioned but lets make sure

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also AC is 2 hours which is way shorter than RPs 25 hours So that means those AC claims are not going through P at all right Thats a key point

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Next part Generate hypotheses for each anomaly Lets tie each hypothesis to the specific anomaly

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For RP anomaly super consistent 25 hour delay low stdev Hypothesis 1 Theres a mandatory service level agreement SLA that requires all claims to be approved within 25 hours of submission so adjusters are rushing to meet the deadline leading to superficial evaluations and skipping of required intermediate steps like proper evaluation to hit the 25 hour mark

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 2 The approval step is partially automated for low value claims with a fixed 25 hour timer that automatically routes claims to approval after submission bypassing manual evaluation steps for certain claim types or amounts

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 3 Theres a data entry error where the timestamp for the P approve activity is hardcoded or manually set to about 25 hours after R rather than being recorded at the actual time of approval leading to artificially low variance

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For PN anomaly 7 day average high stdev Hypothesis 1 The notification step is handled by a small overloaded team that only processes notifications in batch once per week leading to most notifications being delayed 7 days with variance coming from occasional urgent claims that get notified faster or team availability issues

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 2 Theres a manual verification step after approval that requires follow up with third parties before the customer can be notified and the time to complete these follow ups varies widely leading to long and inconsistent delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 3 The notification system has a bug that queues approved claims for 7 days by default with occasional manual overrides that reduce the delay for high priority claims leading to high standard deviation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For AC anomaly 2 hour average closure after assignment low stdev Hypothesis 1 Adjusters are closing claims immediately after assignment if the claim is clearly invalid for example duplicate submission or out of scope without completing evaluation approval or notification steps leading to premature closure

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 2 Theres a process error where claims are accidentally closed when they are assigned to an adjuster due to a faulty workflow configuration that triggers the close activity automatically after assignment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - NE
Text: Hypothesis 3 Fraudulent activity where adjusters are closing claims without processing them to embezzle funds or avoid work and the consistent 2 hour window is the time they take to complete the fraudulent closure after receiving the assignment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For EN anomaly 5 minute average low stdev Hypothesis 1 The approval step is being skipped entirely for most claims with the notify activity triggered immediately after evaluation by an automated workflow meaning claims are approved and notified without human review

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 2 The E and P activity timestamps are being recorded at the same time by adjusters who complete both steps in a single session leading to an artificially short time between E and N since P is unrecorded or merged with E

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis 3 Theres an automated approval rule that automatically approves claims immediately after evaluation if the claim amount is below a certain threshold leading to near instant transition from evaluation to approval to notification

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Okay thats the hypotheses part Now the SQL queries part We need to write PostgreSQL queries right Lets structure each query to address specific verification points tie them to the anomalies

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First for each claim we need to get the timestamp of each activity then compute the differences

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First lets do a query to identify claims where RP time is outside the expected range Wait the temporal profile for RP is avg 90k seconds stdev 3600 so ZETA lets say 3 so acceptable range is 90k plus or minus 3 times 3600 which is 82800 to 97200 seconds which is 23 to 27 hours

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Also we can check if those claims have A and E activities between R and P right

Name: Validation - PE
Text: if RP is 25 hours but theres no A or E thats a problem

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: query that calculates RP time per claim flags those outside 3 stdev and also checks if the intermediate activities A E exist between R and P

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Then we can join this with claims table to get claim type amount adjuster and so on

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Next for the PN anomaly which has avg 604800 seconds stdev 172800 so 3 stdev range is 604800 plus or minus 518400 which is 86400 to 1123200 seconds which is 1 day to 13 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: we want to find claims where PN time is outside that or even within but very long Also we can check if the resource for N is the same as other steps or if its a specific resource region

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: assuming resource for A is adjuster name Or adjuster_id Wait adjusters has adjuster_id name and so maybe join adjusters on the resource for A activity Wait no maybe better to get the adjuster for the claim from the A activity Lets adjust that

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: join adjusters on at assigned_resource equals a name assuming resource is adjuster name matches adjusters name

Name: Validation - PE
Text: it will show which adjusters regions claim types have the longest PN delays

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Next the AC anomaly claims closed within 3 hours of assignment since avg is 2 hours stdev 1 so 1 to 3 hours We need to find those claims and check if they are missing E P N steps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Then we can see if these are concentrated with certain adjusters regions claim types

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then the EN anomaly average 5 minutes stdev 1 minute so acceptable range is 300 plus or minus 3 times 60 which is 120 to 480 seconds 2 to 8 minutes So find claims where EN is less than 2 minutes or check if the P step is missing between E and N

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This will find claims where E to N is too fast or missing the P step entirely Then we can correlate with adjusters claim types and so on

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: For example if all these missing approval steps are for claims under 500 that supports the hypothesis that theres an auto approval rule for small claims

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also maybe add a query to check the variance of RP time across all claims to confirm the low stdev

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Another query to check the distribution of PN times to see if theres a bimodal distribution most at 7 days some much shorter which would explain the high stdev

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Also a query to check if AC claims are being closed by the same resource that assigned them which would support the hypothesis of premature closure or fraudulent activity

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait also for the EN anomaly we can check if the P steps timestamp is the same as E or N which would mean its merged or skipped

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets structure the answer properly first list the anomalies then hypotheses then verification queries making sure its all independent no reference to the instructions

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets make sure theres no reference to the instructions its all standalone Lets check if the hypotheses tie to the anomalies the queries are specific to PostgreSQL use the correct tables and columns Yeah that works

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the schema says resource is VARCHAR so name makes sense The temporal profile uses seconds so the queries use EXTRACT EPOCH FROM interval which gives seconds in PostgreSQL thats correct Yes this should be good
